title: Object Detection Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of bounding boxes, YOLO, NMS, and evaluation metrics for object detection.

questions.question: What does a bounding box in XYWH format represent?

options.text: X-min, Y-min, Width, Height of the top-left corner and box dimensions
X-center, Y-center, Width, Height — the center point and dimensions of the box
X-max, Y-max, Width, Height of the bottom-right corner
X-offset, Y-offset of the box relative to the previous frame in a video

questions.question: What problem does Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) solve in object detection?

options.text: It speeds up the forward pass by skipping empty image regions
It removes duplicate overlapping bounding boxes, keeping only the highest-confidence detection per object
It resizes the input image to the required resolution
It filters out background pixels from the image before detection

questions.question: What is the key architectural difference between YOLO and two-stage detectors like Faster R-CNN?

options.text: YOLO requires more training data than Faster R-CNN
YOLO can only detect one object per image, while Faster R-CNN detects multiple
YOLO processes the image in a single forward pass predicting all boxes at once, while two-stage detectors first propose regions then classify them separately
YOLO only works on GPU while Faster R-CNN can run on CPU

questions.question: What does mAP@50 measure in object detection evaluation?

options.text: Mean accuracy at 50% training completion
The mean of Average Precision across all classes, using an IoU threshold of 0.5 to determine if a detection is correct
The model's speed in processing 50 frames per second
The percentage of images where the model detects at least 50% of objects

questions.question: When fine-tuning a YOLO model on custom data, what is the recommended starting point?

options.text: Always train from random weights (Xavier initialization) for best results on custom tasks
Start from a pretrained model (e.g., trained on COCO) and fine-tune — this requires far less data and converges much faster
Use only the last layer of a pretrained model and freeze all other weights permanently
Download ground truth labels from COCO and merge them with your custom labels
